rulings.Case Title: **TRANSFERRED TO MARIN COUNTY** CONS. OF DONALD PAUL LERA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name and case number. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) Conservator paid Gilfix & La Poll Associates, LLP $1,782.00 without court authority. Receipt filed 11/18/2021 showing the funds returned to conservatorship. $1,500.00 was paid 11/15/2018 unknown when the $282.00 was paid. 2) Conservator used $109.40 in conservatorship funds for her own benefit. Receipt filed 11/18/2021 showing the funds returned to conservatorship. 3) Examiner requested receipts for all entries listed on page 7-9 paragraph 29. 11/18/2021 Supplemental Declaration provides some response. It appears $3,243.00 in receipts have not been provided. Entries: 12/23/2013, 12/19/2013, 08/08/2014, 10/02/2014, 10/6/2014, 12/03/2014,01/10/2015, 03/02/2015, 07/20/2015, 02/29/2016, 03/21/2016, and 04/01/2016. 4) Conservator transferred $5,000.00 from the conservatorship without court authority. Conservator alleges $41.00 in lost interest as a result of the transfers. 5) The starting balance of $20,266.34 does not match the prior account ending balance of $20,266.35. 6) Attorney requests reimbursement for $620.50 in costs which includes $8.00 in parking. [See LRSF 14.58 B.] and $17.50 for waiting by Swift Attorney Services. This entry appears to be a benefit to the attorney rather than the conservatorship.

rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Determine Heirship (Probate Code Section 11700) And For Order To Increase Bond
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 02/08/2022. Continued again same issues. 1) Examiner still does not have the required courtesy copy of the 09/03/2021 Declaration. Once the declaration is received it will be reviewed and there may be other issues. Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. 2) Please explain the the difference is last names on material side for Teresita V. Chong, Leticia J. Gonzalez, Elvira J. Guinto, Josephine Santos, Anges J. Ico. 3) Please file evidence tracing Michael Angelo Sants to Josephine Santos. 4) Please file and explain the Josephine Santos written instrument and provde the legal authority in support. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 01/24/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Neither a petition for final distribution nor a petition for approval of a report of status of administration has been filed. The statutory deadline was 09/14/2020. No final inventory and appraisal is on file. The statutory deadline was 01/13/2020. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CORA MAE WILLIAMS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: APPEARANCE FOR NO RESPONSE: If a hearing is continued for issues raised by an Examiner to be addressed and there is no response prior to the continued hearing date, the matter is automatically continued again, usually for at least one month. If there is still no response, an appearance is required. If no one appears after the automatic second continuance, the matter is ordinarily taken off calendar if it is not a compulsory petition, or an Order to Appear is issued. Numerous issues were posted in the tentative ruling for the hearing on 11/9/21, but they still have not been addressed. Still no courtesy copies of the proof of service, proof of publication, JC form DE-147S, and complete Duties and Liabilities have been received by this Examiner. Additionally, the petition has not been supplemented to disclose who the successors-in-interest are of the post-deceased daughter and whose children the two grandchildren are. Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order on the court's website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE RAMONA M. ALBRIGHT FAMILY TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Verified Petition For Order To Quiet Title And Recover Trust Assets; Undue Influence-Invalidation Of Procured Trust Amendment And Other Documents, And Damages; Fraud-Recission, And Damages; Order For Damages And Attorney'S Fees Under Welfare & Institutions Code Section 15610.30 And Probate Code Section 859; Unjust Entrichment/Restitution; Constructive Trust; Order Preventing Respondent From Using Trust Assets To Defend This Action; Order Appointiing Private Fiduciary As Trustee
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues may include: a. Petitioner requests an order quieting title to 127 Crown, first to the 2013 trust and then in the names of Margaret Consoer and Keith Consoer as beneficiaries. However, 127 Crown appears to already be a trust asset. No allegation is made as to why quieting title is necessary and whether this has been properly presented in probate court. In addition, no showing has been made as to the necessity of quieting title to Margaret Consoer and Keith Consoer or the ability of the court to do so. b. Petitioner requests that the court order Kieran Keaney to return property. A petition under Probate Code 850 must specify the assets to be returned and proper notice on the correct Judicial Council form must be provided. Remote appearances should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Information regarding appearance by CourtCall can be found in the 10th amended general order.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE RAMONA M. ALBRIGHT FAMILY TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues may include: a. Court to determine whether there has been a sufficient showing that Kieran Keaney has taken possession over trust assets to warrant an order to account. b. Petitioner also requests an accounting for unspecified and non-trust assets. Remote appearances should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Information regarding appearance by CourtCall can be found in the 10th amended general order.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE RAMONA M. ALBRIGHT FAMILY TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Remove Trustee And Appoint Successor Trustee To Fill Vacancy
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues may include: a. Petitioner requests appointment as successor trustee but the trust instrument nominates Margaret Consoer to serve, then Keith Consoer. Petitioner avers that Decedent changed the trustee nomination to Gordon Yamamoto in his agreement with Settlor, which suggests that she no longer wanted either to serve. b. Petitioner requests waiver of bond, but waivers of bond have not been filed and Petitioner is not nominated under the trust to serve as trustee. Remote appearances should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Information regarding appearance by CourtCall can be found in the 10th amended general order.

Sec. 8120.] No Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License. [LRSF 14.18 E.] The date of death of any heir must be set forth. [LRSF 14.18 D.] (Parents and siblings are not heirs if a decedent was survived by issue.) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 1/14/22. If notice has not yet been published or more time is needed to prepare and file the responsive pleadings and provide the courtesy copies and proposed order by 1/14/22, Counsel should contact the assigned Examiner to request additional time. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DANA MICHELLE BURNS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The petitioner is a resident of Arizona. The petition requests a $20,000 minimum bond, even though the decedent's liabilities include liens on her real property in the total amount of $703,328, and credit card debts in the total amount of $46,975 for which two civil actions have already been filed by the creditor. The petitioner alleges that the liens should not be considered in determining the amount of his bond because they are secured by the decedent's real property, but It is unclear whether the lienholders intend to waive all recourse against the estate including a deficiency judgment and attorney's fees under Probate Code section 9391 or CCP section 580b. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.
